reasoning: The question requires at least 4 of the 7 Magnificent Seven stocks to simultaneously (or at least within the timeframe) close 50% below their all-time highs before Jan 1, 2026. As of mid-2025, TSLA is the only stock that has seen massive drawdowns >40-50% recently. Most other Mag 7 stocks (AAPL, MSFT, GOOG, AMZN, META, NVDA) experienced phenomenal runs in 2024 and early 2025, setting massive new all-time highs (e.g., META >$650, AMZN >$220, NVDA >$170 post-split). While they have seen a recent 15-25% tech correction in Q1 2025 due to tariff and valuation concerns, they are still trading at very elevated levels compared to 2023. To hit a 50% drawdown from their recent 2025 peaks, they would need to drop another ~35% from their current June 2025 levels. While an "AI bubble pop" or severe recession could trigger this, doing so within the remaining 6.5 months is a tight timeframe. The historical base rate for 35%+ broad mega-cap crashes in a 6-month period is around 5%. Accounting for the community's 20% estimate (likely factoring in AI bubble/tariff risks), I estimate the probability at 12%.
